Noted Behavior: BEST MODEL - Baseline configuration proved most stable; agent learned consistent shooting and movement patterns with the highest mean reward
WORST CNN - High epsilon floor of 0.2 kept agent 20% random throughout; too much randomness prevented consistent exploitation of learned strategies
Fully greedy at end; removing the exploration floor entirely hurt performance - agent committed too hard to early suboptimal strategies
Fast decay to a high floor; snapping to exploitation quickly while staying 15% random compounded negatively
Very high gamma caused agent to over-prioritise distant future rewards; performed better than most epsilon experiments but below baseline
Small batch of 16 introduced noisy gradient updates; high noise from tiny batches limited learning quality
High lr combined with tiny batch caused instability; noisiest CNN experiment - frequent updates on small batches caused weights to oscillate
BEST MLP - Higher lr helped MLP learn faster from RAM observations; larger batch stabilised updates giving the best MLP result
MLP baseline with more timesteps; lower lr slowed learning from RAM state - confirmed MLP needs higher lr than CNN to perform well
std_reward=0.0 suggests all evaluation episodes scored identically - agent likely converged to a degenerate fixed strategy with no variation
Near-zero epsilon floor with slightly faster decay; performed below baseline - 100k timesteps was insufficient for this config to fully converge
